recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — SAME NEW STATION as V28. Vizio monitor (logo visible at upper left) with significant color banding/moire at edges. Same bright red foam. 1280x720 at ~30fps. Very close framing — task area fills frame but monitor artifacts reduce quality. Dark suture (not pink/purple like V28 — possibly different suture or just color rendering). Instruments already visible at frame 1 — recording started after task began or with minimal idle time.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments already at drain. One instrument from lower left, one from right. Dark suture visible extending from drain. Task already in progress. Vizio monitor with moire artifacts.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Recording starts mid-task or immediately at instrument entry. TIMING STARTS ~1-3s.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments at drain. Drain appears to have shifted/tilted. Dark suture loops visible above drain. First throw wrapping or tightening.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 1 minute in, first throw still in progress. Slower than familiar equipment.

frame_analyses.description: Instrument from lower left at drain. Knot building at drain center. Dark suture visible. Still in throw sequence at 2.5 minutes.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Throws extremely prolonged on this equipment. Likely second or third throw.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments crossing at drain. Knot visible with suture tails. Appears to be cutting phase. Instrument from lower left, one from upper right.
Both instruments at drain. Knot at center with suture wrapping. Cutting in progress. Dark suture tails visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 40+ seconds into cutting. Slow but faster than V28's 135s cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Near completion. One instrument remaining at upper left. Knot visible at drain with short tails. Drain appears closed. Drain not avulsed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completion ~295s. Short tails visible. Final product appears acceptable.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw ~55s. Still very slow on new equipment but improved from V28.
Third throw ~60s. Prolonged but consistent with throw 2.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Moire artifacts and close framing make placement assessment nearly impossible. Conservative estimate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: At 300s, drain appears closed with compact knot. Short tails. Drain not avulsed despite 5 minutes of manipulation. Better final product visibility than V28.

technique_summary: V29 at approximately 295 seconds is the second consecutive catastrophic performance on the new equipment station (Vizio monitor, red foam, different instruments). However, it represents a 19% IMPROVEMENT from V28 (365s → 295s), indicating the trainee is beginning to adapt. Throws consumed ~175s (normally 50-67s) and cutting ~95s (normally 26-67s) — both still 2-3x slower than on familiar equipment, but significantly improved from V28's 205s throws and 135s cutting. The dark suture (vs V28's pink/purple) may indicate return to familiar suture material, which could explain part of the improvement. The final product appears acceptable with short tails and closed drain. The V28→V29 improvement trajectory (365→295 = 70s improvement) suggests that with 2-3 more practice runs, the trainee could reach the 150-200s range on this equipment, and eventually return to sub-150s.
improvement_suggestions: V28→V29 improvement of 70 seconds (19%) — ADAPTATION IS OCCURRING on the new equipment.
Throws improved from ~205s (V28) to ~175s (V29) — 15% faster. Still 3x slower than familiar equipment.
Cutting improved from 135s (V28) to 95s (V29) — 30% faster. Still 2x slower than familiar equipment.
At this adaptation rate (~70s improvement per attempt), 2-3 more attempts could bring times to ~150-200s.
The equipment-change adaptation curve is EXPECTED and mirrors the original learning curve (V1-V5 era).
If possible, return to familiar equipment for examination-scored attempts. Use new equipment for practice.
RECORDING: Vizio monitor has severe moire artifacts. Use a polarizing filter or adjust camera angle to reduce.
CONFIDENCE very low (0.30) due to moire, color banding, and close framing obscuring detail.
strengths: 19% improvement from V28 (365s → 295s) — rapid equipment adaptation
Final product appears acceptable — short tails, closed drain
Drain not avulsed — 29 consecutive videos without avulsion
Suture may be dark/standard (vs V28's pink) — partial equipment familiarity restored

key_observations: 295s / 297 FLS — SECOND WORST in series but 19% better than V28 (365s).
DIFFERENT MONITOR: Vizio (not RCA) with significant moire artifacts. Same red foam as V28.
Adaptation occurring: V28 365s → V29 295s = 70s improvement. Equipment learning curve in progress.
Dark suture (vs V28 pink) may mean partial return to familiar materials.
CONFIDENCE: 0.30 — lowest in series due to moire, artifacts, and close framing.
Projected: 2-3 more attempts could reach 150-200s on this equipment.
29th video. V28-V29 should be treated as a SEPARATE EQUIPMENT ARC from V1-V27.

equipment_adaptation_curve.note: Linear projection. Actual adaptation may be faster (power law) or slower (asymptotic).
